channel.title: Openai on Plot Twist Daily
channel.description: Recent content in Openai on Plot Twist Daily

item.title: The AI Pricing Wars Are Just Beginning
item.author: arty.craftson@pottersquill.com (Arty Craftson)
item.description:
Headline

OpenAI announces GPT-5 at 10x the cost of GPT-4. Meanwhile, Anthropic drops Claude prices by 60%. The AI pricing war has officially begun.

Context

The AI industry is at a crossroads. OpenAI just dropped GPT-5 with capabilities that dwarf GPT-4—but at a premium price point that has startups sweating. Simultaneously, Anthropic is playing the long game, slashing prices to capture market share.

Here’s what’s happening:

  • GPT-5 Pro: $200/month for unlimited access
  • Claude 4 Opus: Now 60% cheaper than GPT-4
  • Google Gemini: Bundled free with Workspace
  • xAI Grok: Still trying to find its footing

The big question: Is AI becoming a commodity, or is quality worth the premium?

item.title: OpenAI GPT-5.4 Drops — Enterprise AI Just Got Real
item.author: arty.craftson@pottersquill.com (Arty Craftson)
item.description:
OpenAI GPT-5.4 Drops — Enterprise AI Just Got Real

OpenAI just dropped GPT-5.4, and it’s being called their most powerful model yet for enterprise work.

What’s the Big Deal?

The new model features native computer use capabilities — meaning it can actually interact with software like a human would, rather than just generating text.

Why This Matters

Enterprise AI just crossed a threshold. We’re talking:

  • Autonomous agents that can navigate your software
  • Workflows that execute end-to-end
  • Less hand-holding, more doing

The Plot Twist?

Anthropic just got designated as a “supply chain risk” by the Pentagon. Coincidence? Probably not.
